title: Becke Line Test Simulator
description: Simulated microscope view of a glass fragment in immersion oil. Adjust glass RI, oil RI, and stage height, then interpret the direction the bright Becke line moves to decide whether the glass or the oil has the higher refractive index.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Trace Evidence
Glass Analysis
Refractive Index
Becke Line Test
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — interpret Becke line movement to compare refractive indices.
chapter_title: Glass, Soil, and Physical Trace Evidence

educational.topic: Refractive index comparison by the Becke line method
learningObjectives: Describe the Becke line test procedure for comparing refractive indices.
Interpret the direction the Becke line moves as stage height changes.
Conclude whether glass RI is greater than, less than, or equal to the oil RI.
educational.prerequisites: Refraction basics; reading a numeric scale

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Microscope-view simulation: adjust glass RI, oil RI, and stage-height sliders (or pick an oil preset) and watch the Becke line move; a panel reports the conclusion
pedagogical.assessment: Students predict the Becke line direction for a given RI difference and stage setting, then verify against the simulation
pedagogical.modelType: Qualitative optics model: Becke line offsets toward the higher-RI medium when the stage is raised and fades when RIs match
